info.title: Unified Software Commercialization Platform Public API
info.description: Contract-first public client and admin API. Product, Application, Tenant and User contexts are resolved from verified sessions; identifiers supplied by clients are never authorization facts.

post.summary: Create a product and its official tenant
Authenticate a registered client and resolve trusted Product/Application context
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Client proof and request nonce provide replay control; successful retries may return an equivalent short-lived session.

put.summary: Replace the versioned capability set for an authorized product
put.x-idempotency-exemption: PUT is naturally idempotent and uses the expected_version precondition.

post.summary: Create a technical application under a product

put.summary: Replace exact redirect, origin and deep-link allowlists
put.x-idempotency-exemption: PUT replaces a complete versioned allowlist and is naturally idempotent.

post.summary: Suspend an application without deleting history
Create an agent tenant under an authorized product
Bind a global user as an administrator in one tenant scope
Authenticate a user inside the trusted client context
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Authentication attempts are rate-limited security events and are not business mutations.

post.summary: Start a server-bound external identity flow
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Each call intentionally creates a short-lived one-time state/nonce flow.

post.summary: Exchange a one-time WeChat code using the bound Application configuration
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Provider code and flow_id are single-use replay controls.

post.summary: Link a verified external identity to the current user

delete.summary: Unlink an external identity after recent authentication

post.summary: Rotate a refresh token and issue a new token pair
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Refresh-token rotation has dedicated concurrency and replay semantics.

post.summary: Revoke the current session
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Revoking an already-revoked current session is a stable no-op.

post.summary: Evaluate current product/tenant entitlement using server time
Grant entitlement from an audited source

get.summary: List redacted devices in the resolved scope

post.summary: Atomically check entitlement/device limit and bind an installation
post.x-idempotency-exemption: client_request_id and installation key identity are the module-defined idempotency controls.

post.summary: Prove installation possession and refresh online/offline lease state
post.x-idempotency-exemption: Server nonce is single-use; retries return a stable result without extending twice.

post.summary: Revoke a device binding in the current scope
Generate a scoped activation-code batch
Create a short-lived one-time delivery artifact
Suspend a batch without revoking historical grants
Claim an activation code and start recoverable entitlement orchestration
post.x-idempotency-exemption: product_id plus client_request_id is the module-defined idempotency key.

get.summary: Read a redacted redemption result in the current scope
List currently sellable offers and server-selected prices

post.summary: Create an offer and draft version
Publish immutable offer and price versions
Create an order from a server-issued catalog snapshot

get.summary: List orders for the current Product/Tenant/User scope
Read one order in the current scope

post.summary: Cancel a still-unpaid order through its state machine
Create a payment intent from the server-side payable order summary
Create short-lived safe payment-launch parameters
Start an audited asynchronous refund
Invoke a versioned logical model through the AI Gateway
post.description: The server resolves Provider, provider model, credentials, route version and price version. For SSE, exactly one terminal event reports completed, cancelled or failed settlement state.
post.x-idempotency-exemption: product scope plus client_request_id is the AI/Usage idempotency boundary and prevents duplicate settlement.

put.summary: Create an immutable logical-model route version

post.summary: Create an immutable metering price version

get.summary: List metered usage and safe billing details in the resolved scope

put.summary: Create or replace a scoped administrator role binding

get.summary: Search redacted audit events inside the authorized scope

ClientSessionBearer.description: Short-lived client session containing server-resolved ProductContext and ApplicationContext.

UserBearer.description: User session bound to trusted Product/Application and server-resolved Tenant context.

AdminBearer.description: Administrator session; every target product/tenant is re-authorized by Access Control.

DeveloperKey.name: Authorization
DeveloperKey.description: Restricted developer key. The key resolves Product/Tenant/scope server-side and cannot select arbitrary contexts.

RequestId.name: X-Request-Id
RequestId.description: Optional caller correlation ID; not an authorization or idempotency fact.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Opaque operation-scoped idempotency key. Reuse with a different payload returns conflict.

ProductId.name: product_id
ProductId.description: Target resource only; access is independently authorized from the admin session.

TenantId.name: tenant_id
TenantId.description: Target resource only; access is independently authorized from the admin session.

AiResponse.description: Final JSON response or an SSE stream with one terminal event

schema.description: SSE events use versioned JSON data; the terminal event contains request_id, terminal status and usage_settlement_ref.

client_proof.description: Versioned proof bound to the registered client; never a permanent embedded secret.

launch_parameters.description: Channel-minimized short-lived parameters; never contains reusable provider credentials.

max_budget_hint.description: Non-authoritative client ceiling hint in the product's configured quota unit.

rollout_scope.description: Authorized product/application rollout constraints; never credentials.

cost_amount_integer.description: Returned only when the current authorization scope may view internal cost.
